acidic_vhs_decay.explanation: Emulates acidic, organic VHS decay processes with magnetic color bleeding and rolling analog synchronization loss. A five-family glitch state controller cycling through VHS tracking, CRT phosphor, digital datamosh, film gate weave, and op-art moiré — each dominating through squared-weight exaggeration while a locked 7-color candy palette rotates via triple-sample chromatic drift, UVs warping through block smears, horizontal tears, rolling bars, lens funnels, and phase displacement, with post-signal overlays of scratches, packet loss, snow, scanlines, and phosphor triads, punctuated by stochastic rupture bursts.
acidic_vhs_decay.technicalDetails: 60fps 1080x1080 via Three.js WebGL2 GLSL 3.0. Five competing damage families: VHS tracking (w_vhs), CRT phosphor (w_crt), digital datamosh (w_dig), film gate weave (w_flm), op-art moiré (w_opa). pow(w, 2.0) weight exaggeration. Strict 7-color palette lock with smoothstep(0.45, 0.55) sharp thresholds. Triple-sample chromatic drift with offset UVs. Polar/Cartesian hybrid coordinates.
acidic_vhs_decay.fieldNotes: No AI, pure deterministic code. Same timestamp = same damage profile. Colors cycle: hot pink, pastel pink, bright teal, electric yellow, saturated purple, neon green, white.

prismatic_tape_oracle.explanation: A three-pass generative system in raw WebGL/Three.js. Pass One: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on floating-point framebuffer — two chemicals (A activator, B inhibitor) diffuse and react with oscillating feed/kill rates. Pass Two: raymarched SDF scene of floating browser panels and asemic UI glyphs drifting through an op-art warped tunnel. Pass Three: luminous risograph with three halftone screens (15/45/75 degrees) mapping to hot pink, neon cyan, acid yellow on deep plum base. VHS tracking wobble, chromatic aberration, localized dropout noise.
prismatic_tape_oracle.technicalDetails: Three-pass pipeline: Sim > Render+Afterimage > Post. 5-point Laplacian stencil. 40-step raymarch with camera at z=-3.0. sdBox browser panels with mod drift. op-art sinusoid warping. Risograph 75 LPI tri-screen. Tracking wobble at 15% speed. Chromatic aberration ±0.008 UV. I-frame injection at 3%. Ping-pong FBO feedback.
prismatic_tape_oracle.fieldNotes: Not clean. Found. Hard color clamps: min (0.08, 0.0, 0.2) even shadows carry purple; max (1.0, 0.9, 0.95) highlights stay warm. No pure white, no pure black. Saturated middle-earth.

spectral_candy_avalanche_garden.explanation: GPU-side Abelian sandpile with incommensurate wandering emitters drives a 24x24 shader-based Wave Function Collapse grid whose truchet arcs and geomantic dot-patterns are colored by golden-angle OKLab palettes and grain-driven spectral interference, all accumulating into complementary afterimage ghosts piped through CRT barrel distortion, chromatic aberration, scanlines, phosphor masking, and ACES tone mapping — a candy-colored avalanche garden growing from cellular automata physics.
spectral_candy_avalanche_garden.technicalDetails: Abelian sandpile Bak-Tang-Wiesenfeld on GPU. floor(grains/4.0) topple redistribution. Ping-pong HalfFloat FBOs. Two aperiodic Lissajous emitters. 24x24 WFC with hash-driven collapse. Truchet dual quarter-circle arcs with mirror flip. 16 European geomantic figures. Golden-angle hue sequencing 137.5077 degrees. Afterimage at 8% mix with OKLab inversion. ACES tonemap.
spectral_candy_avalanche_garden.fieldNotes: Garden grows, collapses, rebirths. ~10s full collapse then reset. Mouse injects grains. Space reseeds. Toggle palettes with 'c', geomancy 'g', CRT 'p'.

led_pulse.explanation: Physical LED subpixel matrix where chromatic moiré at three mismatched scales produces breathing rosettes, phase-locked to a 60 BPM heartbeat driving systolic VHS tears, diastolic datamosh blocks, hot-pixel sensor damage, deep violet scan bands — all in fully saturated hot pink, acid lime, electric cobalt with interference-derived bloom.
led_pulse.technicalDetails: 5-pixel RGB triads with anti-aliased subpixel masks. Three grids at 1/5.1, 1/5.14, 1/5.18 with opposing rotations. 60 BPM: tBeat=fract(u_time), systolic exp(-tBeat*20), diastolic exp(-fract(tBeat-0.2)*20). Systolic VHS tear at 0.4Hz. Diastolic 12x8 block datamosh. Hot pixels at 0.998 threshold 15Hz flicker. Deep violet scan at 35x frequency. Phosphor compression pow(pixelColor, 0.9).
led_pulse.fieldNotes: Every 'pixel' is three separate physical emitters with black dead space. The subpixel structure IS the image. Simulates actual display hardware with individual LEDs dying and resurrecting.

magenta_glitch_tape.explanation: Chromatic moiré from two drifting crossing line-grids at 45 and 48 degrees with per-channel recomputation, VHS tracking tears, head-switching bottom noise, magenta-biased palette, 1.5x scanlines, color-harmonized grain, luma-driven phosphor bloom — a woven magenta glitch tapestry breathing through trigonometric math.
magenta_glitch_tape.technicalDetails: Two grids 45deg/48deg, 3deg offset, scale mismatch 45/46. Per-channel chromatic separation — full grid math recomputed per channel. Two-threshold VHS tear. Head-switching bottom 8%. Four palette stops: dark purple, deep violet, hot pink, magenta grain. 1.5x scanlines. Luma bloom via Rec.601. Optical vignette.
magenta_glitch_tape.fieldNotes: Not post-process RGB split — moiré fringes themselves physically separated in color space. Even noise is color-harmonized, no grayscale anywhere.

feral_feedback_loop.explanation: OKLab-perceptual feedback organism where Lenia cellular automata breathe through hyperbolic dream physics, op-art moiré, asemic browser debris, printed through Risograph halftone, smeared by datamosh motion vectors, scarred by VHS tear and colored dropout — a dark psychedelic feedback loop.
feral_feedback_loop.technicalDetails: Ping-pong HalfFloat feedback. Lenia/Brain's Brain hybrid CA with Gaussian growth. Dream Physics: Kairotempics nonlinear time, Mnemonic Gravity spiral suction, Hyperbolic Folding recursive depth. Central Oracle op-art ring 60x/8x frequency. sdBox browser panels mod-wrapped. Op-art moiré: 40x radial sine vs 30x diagonal cosine. Chromatic aberration per-channel offsets. VHS tear + tracking wobble. Datamosh temporal drag with luma motion vectors. Risograph 90 LPI halftone. 1% colored dropout.
feral_feedback_loop.fieldNotes: Feedback blend oscillates 0.7+0.2*sin(u_time*0.4) — echo breathes between persistence and responsiveness. Piece remembers more in quiet moments, forgets faster during chaos.

computational_soup.explanation: 6-layer volumetric soup: acoustic-impedance FBM warps Lenia-pulsed Voronoi grid, Chladni nodes and moiré modulate Michel-Lévy birefringence, Abelian sandpile quantization snaps colors to crystalline bands, prism dispersion splits wavelengths per depth layer, impossible Stygian Blue/Self-Luminous Red push non-physical color space — acid palette with halation bloom and 16mm film grain.
computational_soup.technicalDetails: 3-octave FBM rotation matrix (0.8,0.6,-0.6,0.8). 3x3 Voronoi with Lenia pulse 2pi/cell. Chladni: cos(px*5)cos(py*5)-cos(px*8)cos(py*8). Moiré 25x/28x counter-rotating. Sandpile quantization to 5 levels. 6 depth slices progressive scale+rotation. Prism dispersion per depth. Michel-Lévy birefringence. Lenia growth density. Stygian Blue + Self-Luminous Red impossible colors. Halation bloom. 16mm clumped grain.
computational_soup.fieldNotes: Bureaucratic Failure: quantization stutters with periodic micro-jumps. Everything mixed in volumetric layers. Temporal polyphony at three simultaneous time scales.

rainblown_rainbow.explanation: Domain-colored Möbius transform drives rainblown anisotropic warp through gyroid and gematria-resonance substrate, thin-film structural color and golden-angle OKLab rainbows blending via thermal streaks, raindrop scattering, chromatic rupture glitches, found-ground grain — sacred-geometry oil slick in perceptually uniform color space.
rainblown_rainbow.technicalDetails: OKLab>linear>sRGB pipeline. Möbius: z²+c / z-c with four orbiting coefficients. 6-octave FBM anisotropic rotation. Rainblown warp: wind (-0.8,-1.2), 3-stage FBM cascade. Gyroid: sin(x)cos(y)+sin(y)cos(t*0.5)+sin(t*0.5)cos(x). Gematria: YHWH 26/15, ELOHIM 86/30, LOGOS 373/100. Thin-film 250-1000nm. rainblown_rainbow.fieldNotes: Möbius poles drift recursively creating infinite winding. Three simultaneous time scales: slow drift, medium flow, fast shimmer. Sacred geometry made signal.

phosphor_reef_engine.technicalDetails: 64-step sphere tracing SDF. Octahedron core with sine displacement. Torus with cuttlefish ridging sin(x*30+t*10)*0.02. Internet debris mod-wrapped. Chromatophore 6x6 grid, 1.24 expansion ratio. Autostereogram depth shift with optics equation. Datamosh: ping-pong HalfFloat, 10% explosive displacement, glitchcore channel swap. feral_hyperbolic_gyroid.technicalDetails: 6-octave domain-warping with hyperbolic/Poincare tear: q += shift*(0.2 + 0.1/(r2+0.05)). Golden-ratio phi=1.618 phase stacking for aperiodic interference. Three scales: slow 0.08x, medium 0.40x, fast 2.50x. Gyroid: abs(sin(q.x)*cos(q.y))*amp, 0.65 decay. CMY misregistration per channel. Screen blend: 1-(1-a)*(1-b). Blinn-Phong exponent 24. Fibrous cross-hatch 80x. CRT: RGB shadow mask + slot mask + scanlines. Electrostatic grain 150x UV tied to t_fast.
feral_hyperbolic_gyroid.fieldNotes: 1/(r2+0.05) creates infinite density folds approaching origin — pole at zero. CMY subtractive primaries instead of additive RGB. Screen blending makes overlaps glow white-hot. Three coordinate systems coexist: polar, raw UV, gl_FragCoord.

acidic_candy.explanation: Full OKLCh fungal texture on Ashima simplex noise, two-layer mycelial domain warping, I-Ching 6-bit bitwise fossilization, ridge-and-decay field mixing, anisotropic gradient-directed chromatic aberration with per-channel time delays, hot-pink halation in crevices, CRT scanlines, purple shadow lift, radial vignette — toxic neon candy biological surface breathing and corrupting in real time.
acidic_candy.technicalDetails: Ashima/IQ snoise() permute() Skewed/Simplex lattice. OKLCh Björn Ottosson full transform. I-Ching: (ip.x ^ ip.y) & 63 deterministic hexagram patterns. Two-layer fbm warp: q=snoise(p*1.5+t*0.15), r=snoise(p*3+q+t*0.1), warpedP=p+r*0.4. Ridge: pow(1-abs(snoise(warpedP*4-t*0.1)),3). Decay: snoise(warpedP*8+q*3)*0.5+0.5. Anisotropic CA: central differences eps=0.015, per-channel time delays. Halation smoothstep(0.55,0.95,v)*0.6 hot pink/magenta. CRT scanlines. Purple shadow lift.
acidic_candy.fieldNotes: I-Ching field: bitwise-coordinate logic producing structured glitch. Mycelial anastomosis via domain warping producing fungal networks. Biology corrupted by binary.

acid_iching.explanation: 3D Ashima simplex noise driving 6-iteration I-Ching hexagram field with bureaucratic terrace quantization, binary yin-yang thresholding, XOR-Ghost Manifold recursive spatial warping, 64-state acid candy palette injection, Sobel-normal PBR dual orbiting lights, thin-film Fresnel on changing-line transitions, hash sparkle on volatile nodes, subsurface valley scattering — Book of Changes as living glowing wet candy resin surface.
acid_iching.technicalDetails: 3D snoise time as 3rd dimension. 6 yao iterations. Bureaucratic quantization: mix(n, floor(n*3+0.5)/3, 0.6). Binary threshold smoothstep(-0.05, 0.05, n) -> 6-bit hexagram ID. XOR-Ghost Manifold: warp_angle = bit*PI + (mod(hex_id,8)/8)*2PI. Acid candy: hot pink, toxic lime, electric cyan, sour yellow. PBR: Sobel normals, Blinn-Phong 64/32. Thin-film Fresnel pow(1-max(dot(N,V),0), 4). Subsurface valley scattering. ACES tonemap.
acid_iching.fieldNotes: 64 hexagrams = 64 color states. 6 yao = 6 nested spatial frequencies. Changing lines = iridescent zones predicting transformation. Each hexagram literally reshapes space around it.

sacred_geometry.technicalDetails: 5-octave fbm with rotation matrix. Golden Spiral: uv *= rot(log(r)/(log(PHI)/(PI/2))*0.3 - t). Hex grid: Flower-of-Life axial vec2(1,SQRT3). Birefringent domain warp dual fbm. Photoelastic stress: fbm(id*0.15-t*2)+r. Thin-film n_film=1.56 Snell correction 300-700nm. Fake 3D normals from SDF. Metatron lines smoothstep(0.04,0.01,d_edge). sacred_geometry.fieldNotes: Golden Spiral locks to PHI — every point rotates by log-distance from center. Chitin refractive index 1.56 from insect exoskeletons. Zero pure-black voids enforced — piece is always luminous.

colorful_moire.technicalDetails: Phantom Spiral: phase = a*arms + log(r+0.001)*tightness + rot. Two spirals multiply smoothstep-gated. Tightness breathes 35+sin(t*0.2)*10. Glitchcore anchor: mouse-following 0.6-radius beauty zone. 25-cell macroblock chew hash-gated packet loss. RGB orbital offsets: red (sin(t*1.1),cos(t*0.8))*0.08, green (cos(t*1.3),sin(t*0.9))*0.08, blue (sin(t*0.7),cos(t*1.2))*0.08. Per-channel moiré own spiral params. Hyperpop: deep purple, hot pink, electric cyan, pearl white. Echo chromatic phase lag red+0.004/blue-0.004. Stutter decay 0.97 at hash>0.95. 8-bit: floor(color*24)/24.
colorful_moire.fieldNotes: Anchor decouples viewer from chaos — protected circle where spiral renders clean. Echo chromatically separated — ghost trails smear in RGB as they fade. Three interleaved moiré patterns beat against each other.

haunted_monitor.fieldNotes: CRT monitors literally remember — phosphor persistence is a form of memory. This piece pushes that to supernatural territory.

moire_star.explanation: Radial moire interference forming star-like patterns — two or more overlapping periodic structures creating emergent geometric blooms. Pure trigonometric math producing optical artifacts without textures.
moire_star.technicalDetails: Multiple radial sine waves at slightly different frequencies multiplied together. Rotating coordinate frames per layer. Smoothstep gating for sharp bands. OKLCh palette mapped to interference intensity. Optional domain warp for organic variation.
moire_star.fieldNotes: Moire is not a bug — it's a physical phenomenon emerging from interference. The star shapes are mathematical facts, not designed shapes.

psychedelic_autostereogram.explanation: Autostereogram (Magic Eye) with psychedelic coloring — repeating wallpaper pattern with embedded 3D depth map, colored by OKLCh spectral palette. The brain extracts hidden depth from flat pattern.
psychedelic_autostereogram.technicalDetails: Autostereogram: repeating wallpaper with depth-map-driven horizontal shifts. OKLCh spectral coloring. Depth map from SDF or fractal. Repeat period ~32px. Cross-eyed or parallel viewing required to perceive depth.
psychedelic_autostereogram.fieldNotes: The 3D image exists only in your brain — the screen is flat. Your visual system fuses the repeating patterns and constructs depth. A collaboration between math and perception.

signal_decay.explanation: Signal lifecycle from transmission to dissolution — clean waveform generated, transmitted, progressively corrupted by noise, interference, dropout, until only static remains. A memento mori for data.
signal_decay.technicalDetails: Clean signal generation: sine/sawtooth/composite. Transmission stage: minimal corruption. Progressive decay stages: noise injection, interference fringes, packet loss, dropout, full static. Each stage weighted and mixed. CRT/phosphor post.
signal_decay.fieldNotes: All signals eventually decay into noise. The static on an untuned TV is the cosmic microwave background — the afterglow of the Big Bang. The universe itself is a fading signal.

vibrant_diffusion.explanation: Vibrant reaction-diffusion patterns — Turing morphogenesis at high saturation, with multiple chemical species creating competing pattern scales. Living wallpaper in toxic neon.
vibrant_diffusion.technicalDetails: Multi-species reaction-diffusion ( activator-inhibitor + substrate). Pattern scale competition: large spots vs fine filaments. OKLCh vibrant palette at high chroma. Temporal evolution. Phosphor glow + scanlines.
vibrant_diffusion.fieldNotes: Alan Turing discovered this math in 1952 — the same equations that build biological patterns. leopard spots, zebra stripes, coral branching. Life literally runs on reaction-diffusion.

vivid_glitch_decay.explanation: Vivid chromatic decay — a signal starting at full saturation progressively losing color information through digital corruption, ending in monochrome static. The death of color.
vivid_glitch_decay.technicalDetails: Full-color signal with progressive desaturation stages. Chromatic aberration increases as signal degrades. Per-channel corruption at different rates. Final stage: true monochrome noise. OKLCh saturation mapped to degradation stage.
vivid_glitch_decay.fieldNotes: Color death is not uniform — some channels die faster than others. The final moments of a dying signal are the most visually interesting.

vivid_glitchcore.explanation: Glitchcore aesthetic at maximum saturation — aggressive digital corruption, macroblock tearing, chroma separation, scanline interference, all in eye-searing OKLCh neon. Not subtle, not polite.
vivid_glitchcore.technicalDetails: Macroblock-level corruption with hash-driven displacement. Aggressive chroma separation: R/G/B independently offset and warped. Scanline subtraction at high intensity. Quantization banding. Inverted colors in corruption zones. OKLCh maximum chroma.
vivid_glitchcore.fieldNotes: Glitchcore as genre: the aesthetics of digital failure weaponized as visual style. Not a bug, the whole point.

volvox_disco_reactor.explanation: Spherical colony organisms (volvox) as bioluminescent disco balls — rotating green spheres with internal daughter colonies, phosphor glow, and chromatic aberration. Biology meets dance floor.
volvox_disco_reactor.technicalDetails: Volvox SDF: hollow sphere with internal daughter spheres. Rotation animation. Bioluminescent green palette with internal glow. Phosphor persistence for trail effects. Chromatic aberration. Bloom. Scanlines.
volvox_disco_reactor.fieldNotes: Volvox are real colonial algae — each 'individual' is actually hundreds of cells living together. They literally have daughter colonies inside. Nature invented the disco ball.

dynamic_fungi.explanation: Procedurally generated fungal networks growing in real time — mycelial branching patterns, spore release, and colony expansion rendered in organic OKLCh tones with phosphor glow.
dynamic_fungi.technicalDetails: Mycelial growth simulation via branching random walk. Spore generation and dispersal. Colony expansion with nutrient competition. OKLCh organic palette: browns, greens, purples. Phosphor glow for bioluminescent effect. Temporal evolution.
dynamic_fungi.fieldNotes: Fungal networks are the largest organisms on Earth — one honey fungus colony covers 2,385 acres. The mycelium is nature's internet, connecting trees in a wood-wide web.

living_fabric.explanation: Fabric that breathes — woven textile pattern with organic distortion, per-thread color variation, and analog damage. Cloth as living, corrupted digital surface.
living_fabric.technicalDetails: Woven grid with sinusoidal thread displacement. Per-thread hash-driven color. Organic warp via fbm. Analog damage: tracking tears, chroma bleed, scanlines. OKLCh textile palette.
living_fabric.fieldNotes: Textiles are the original programmable material — weaving is computation with thread. This piece makes that explicit.

cobalt_scan.explanation: Cobalt-blue scanning beam — horizontal raster sweep with phosphor excitation, chromatic decay, and CRT artifacts. The physics of electron beams hitting phosphor, rendered in deep electric blue.
cobalt_scan.technicalDetails: Horizontal raster sweep simulation. Phosphor excitation and decay per pixel. Cobalt-dominant palette: deep blue, electric blue, cyan, white hot spots. Scanline frequency matching. Bloom on bright excitation points. Chroma separation on trailing edge.
cobalt_scan.fieldNotes: A CRT electron beam is literally shooting electrons at phosphor-coated glass. The phosphor atoms absorb the energy and re-emit it as light. This piece simulates that physical process.

lenia_lace.explanation: Lenia continuous cellular automaton producing lace-like organic patterns — fine filigree structures emerging from smooth growth kernels, rendered in delicate OKLCh pastels with phosphor glow.
lenia_lace.technicalDetails: Lenia CA with fine-scale growth kernel producing filament/lace morphology. Multiple kernel rings for complex branching. OKLCh pastel palette: soft pinks, lavenders, creams. Phosphor glow for ethereal quality. Scanlines + vignette.
lenia_lace.fieldNotes: Lenia at fine scales produces structures that look like coral, lace, or vascular networks. The same math at different parameters produces completely different morphologies.

lenia_velvet_damask.explanation: Lenia CA producing velvet damask patterns — rich repeating ornamental structures with deep shadows and highlights, like woven silk rendered as living cellular automata.
lenia_velvet_damask.technicalDetails: Lenia CA parameters tuned for large-scale ornamental patterns. Growth kernel producing repeating motifs. OKLCh deep palette: burgundy, gold, forest green, navy. Shadow/highlight simulation via pseudo-lighting. Phosphor glow. Scanlines.
lenia_velvet_damask.fieldNotes: Damask is a reversible figured fabric — the pattern is woven in, not printed. This piece simulates that woven quality through cellular automata rules.

rd_lace_sampler.explanation: Reaction-diffusion lace sampler — multiple RD patterns side by side or overlaid: spots, stripes, labyrinth, worms, each with distinct OKLCh coloring. A catalog of Turing morphogenesis.
rd_lace_sampler.technicalDetails: Multiple RD simulations with different feed/kill parameters: spots, stripes, labyrinth, worms. Side-by-side or layered composition. Per-pattern OKLCh coloring. Phosphor glow. Scanlines + vignette.
rd_lace_sampler.fieldNotes: Different feed/kill rates in Gray-Scott produce completely different patterns — it's a parameter space of biological forms. This piece samples that space.

rainbow_fractal.explanation: Rainbow-colored fractal exploration — Mandelbrot, Julia, or Newton iterations with spectral/hue-cycled coloring and phosphor post-processing. Mathematical infinity in prismatic color.
rainbow_fractal.technicalDetails: Standard fractal iteration (Mandelbrot/Julia/Newton). Hue cycled by iteration count or smooth coloring. OKLCh spectral palette. Phosphor glow + bloom. CRT scanlines + vignette.
rainbow_fractal.fieldNotes: Fractals are infinite detail at finite boundaries — you can zoom forever and never exhaust the pattern. Rainbow coloring reveals the iteration structure.

gyroid_chroma_engine.explanation: Gyroid minimal surface as chromatic engine — triply periodic structure dividing space into two interpenetrating channels, colored by OKLCh spectral flow. Mathematical biology as generative substrate.
gyroid_chroma_engine.technicalDetails: Gyroid SDF: sin(x)cos(y) + sin(y)cos(z) + sin(z)cos(x). Triply periodic minimal surface. OKLCh spectral coloring by position. Domain warp for organic variation. Phosphor glow. Bloom + scanlines.
gyroid_chroma_engine.fieldNotes: The gyroid is found in butterfly wings, cell membranes, and block copolymers. Nature loves this shape because it maximizes surface area with minimal material.

geocities_cathedral.explanation: GeoCities-era web aesthetics as cathedral architecture — tiled backgrounds, animated GIFs, visitor counters, and MIDI visualizers rendered as sacred digital space. Nostalgia as religion.
geocities_cathedral.technicalDetails: GeoCities visual vocabulary: tiled patterns, starfield backgrounds, animated elements, construction gifs. Cathedral architecture via SDF composition. Visitor counter as digital rosary. OKLCh retro palette. CRT simulation. Heavy scanlines.
geocities_cathedral.fieldNotes: GeoCities was the original creative web — millions of people building personal shrines to their interests. This piece treats that era with the reverence usually reserved for Gothic cathedrals.

emerald_incal.explanation: Moebius/Jodorowsky Incal-inspired emerald city — crystalline architecture in green/cyan spectrum, raymarched SDF scene with phosphor glow and chromatic aberration. Sci-fi cathedral in toxic emerald.
emerald_incal.technicalDetails: Raymarched SDF architectural forms: towers, bridges, domes. Emerald/cyan OKLCh palette with gold accents. Chromatic aberration for psychedelic quality. Phosphor glow. Bloom. Scanlines. Slow camera drift.
emerald_incal.fieldNotes: The Incal is a seminal sci-fi comic by Moebius and Jodorowsky — surreal architecture, cosmic mysticism, impossible geometry. This piece channels that energy.

tarot_math.explanation: Tarot archetypes as mathematical structures — major arcana rendered as SDF compositions with symbolic geometry, colored by OKLCh palette. Divination made geometric.
tarot_math.technicalDetails: Tarot symbols via SDF composition: circles, lines, stars, crosses. Per-card symbolic geometry. OKLCh palette varying by card suit/element. Phosphor glow. Bloom. Scanlines. Temporal evolution.
tarot_math.fieldNotes: Tarot = archetypal images that trigger pattern recognition. Mathematics = pattern made explicit. This piece fuses divination and computation.

tibetan_rainbow_math.explanation: Tibetan Buddhist rainbow body as mathematical visualization — spectral colors arranged in traditional thangka composition, with procedural mandala elements and phosphor glow. Sacred geometry from Himalayan tradition.
tibetan_rainbow_math.technicalDetails: Rainbow body: five pure lights (blue, green, red, yellow, white) representing elements. Mandala structure: concentric circles, cardinal gates, center deity. Procedural generation via polar coordinates. OKLCh spectral palette. Phosphor glow. Bloom.
tibetan_rainbow_math.fieldNotes: The rainbow body is the highest attainment in Tibetan Buddhism — the transformation of the physical body into light. This piece visualizes that concept through generative math.

chroma_soup.explanation: Chromatic soup — multiple color mixing systems overlaid: spectral wavelength mapping, OKLCh palette cycling, thin-film interference, all stirred together in a volumetric broth. Color theory as generative system.
chroma_soup.technicalDetails: Multiple color systems: spectral wavelength->RGB via Gaussian lobes, OKLCh palette cycling, thin-film interference. Volumetric layering with depth-dependent mixing. Domain warp for organic flow. Phosphor glow. Bloom.
chroma_soup.fieldNotes: Color is not a property of objects — it's a collaboration between light, material, and perception. This piece makes that collaboration visible.
